evals.prompt: Refactor OrderService.java to follow our Java 17 coding conventions. The class has mutable state, uses loops instead of streams, throws checked exceptions, uses null returns, and builds a report via string concatenation. Use records, streams, Optional, switch expressions, text blocks, Stream.toList(), final var, and List.of/copyOf where appropriate.
evals.expected_output: Refactored OrderService with: final fields, constructor injection, streams instead of loops, Optional instead of null, unchecked exceptions, small focused methods, final var locals, Stream.toList()/List.copyOf for immutable results, text block for the report template, no post-Java 17 features.

assertions.text: All local variables and parameters use final (final var allowed)
No checked exceptions in method signatures
No for/while loops — all collection processing uses streams
Methods that may not find a result return Optional instead of null
No method exceeds ~20 lines of logic

evals.prompt: Refactor UserProfile.java to follow our Java 17 conventions. It's a mutable JavaBean with getters/setters and imperative collection code. Convert it to a record (records are the idiomatic choice for data carriers in Java 17). Replace the imperative hasRole / getActiveRoles methods with stream-based implementations, and defensively copy the roles list in the compact constructor.
evals.expected_output: UserProfile is a record with a compact constructor that uses List.copyOf for defensive copy. hasRole and getActiveRoles use streams. No setters. No post-Java 17 features.

evals.prompt: Write a Java 17 payment processing module in package com.example.payment. Define a sealed interface PaymentResult permitting three record implementations: Success(String transactionId), Declined(String reason), and Error(String message). Then write a PaymentDispatcher class with a single method 'describe(PaymentResult result)' that returns a human-readable String. Use instanceof pattern matching (pattern matching for switch is NOT available in Java 17). Use final var locals and final pattern variables.
evals.expected_output: sealed interface PaymentResult with three record subtypes, PaymentDispatcher.describe using instanceof pattern matching with final pattern variables. No post-Java 17 features.

assertions.text: Uses instanceof pattern matching with final pattern variables
All local variables and parameters use final (final var allowed)
